outcomes.note: Semifinals not yet played as of this pass (FRA v ESP pre-kickoff Jul 14). Fill in on Run 3.

carriedForwardFromRun1: All Run 1 verified facts and corrections stand unchanged: Opta post-QF figures, Silver paywall note, penalty superlative softening, Cape Verde views dropped, Xhaka paraphrase, Grondona as 'Soccer Official #1', TourProdEnter $260M/$57M attributed to La Nación, Grimes/Garicano/precedent confirmations, VAR 2026 second-yellow provision.
newSinceRun1: Bracket unchanged: still pre-semis. FRA v ESP had not kicked off at pass time; ENG v ARG is Jul 15. Opta re-verified Jul 14 — identical (34.0/23.4/21.9/20.6; reach-final 57.7/42.3/50.9/49.1).
Financial thread deepened (still allegation, no charges): TourProdEnter LLC allegedly routed AFA revenue through FIVE named US banks — Citibank, Bank of America, JPMorgan Chase, PNC Bank, Synovus.
Miami Herald reports investigators have traced ~$90M in 'potentially irregular' uses of funds to date — distinct from and larger than La Nación's $57M-unexplained figure.

Guillermo Tofoni (Argentine sports businessman, former AFA commercial handler) reportedly gave a ~3-hour videoconference interview to FBI agents + DOJ prosecutors about how AFA structured its international commercial agreements. Run 1 flagged him only as a potential witness; he has now reportedly been interviewed. Witness, not charged. Match-timing of the session conflicts across outlets → not pinned.
TourProdEnter operator described as a 'former legislator and businessman' (+ spouse) — kept by role, not name.
AFA pushback added: a representative said investigative steps alone 'do not determine responsibility or guilt' (single secondary source, kept short/attributed).

Squad news for the semis: no suspensions (cards wiped after QFs). ARG — Messi fit after an eye knock (played 120'), Romero a fitness doubt. ENG — Rice expected to start after illness, Henderson doubtful (broke arm celebrating R16). FRA — Mbappé's precautionary knock eased. ESP — no notable concerns.
Bookmaker drift (immaterial): France still clear favorite (~+140/+150); England and Spain now near-level (~+310/+340); Argentina drifted to longest (~+360/+430). Order unchanged.
dataChangesThisRun: models.ts BOOKMAKERS refreshed asOf 2026-07-14: ESP +330->+320 (implied 0.238), ARG +400->+430 (implied 0.188); source note updated; England/Spain near-level noted. Opta + Silver blocks unchanged.
